steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The opening sentence establishes the thesis: “initiatives that rely on natural features or processes can help address such challenges.” The blueback salmon example illustrates this principle in action—showing how a nature-based intervention (restoring logjams) solves a conservation problem.

steps.title: Match the claim to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D restates the passage's central claim directly: nature-based approaches work as conservation tools.

A narrows the focus to partnership and collaboration, which the passage mentions but doesn't emphasize as its main point. B zeroes in on a single detail (logjams help salmon spawn) rather than the broader lesson the passage teaches. C invents a comparison between nature-based and non-nature-based methods and attributes a new realization to scientists—neither claim appears in the text.

The passage demonstrates that a nature-based method succeeded at conservation, proving the opening premise. That matches choice D — D is correct.
